mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in >60% of observations during easing periods, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered observations, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05) across multiple rolling windows, or returns are negative in >60% of expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirms a technical recession, prompting equity deleveraging and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: The recession signal fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20-day forward returns occurring in >55% of triggered cases over historical recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment signal synchronized supply-side expansion, boosting market sentiment and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal underperforms independent proxies, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of synchronized expansion periods during walk-forward testing.
